section: # -- client_income.csv: Work income, self-employment, retirement contributions, and pension/annuity income streams.

label: annual_earned_income
notes: Gross in earn_start_year

label: earned_income_last_year
notes: Base; extend via Scenarios > Retire Later

label: earned_income_annual_increase
notes: Growth rate for base years

label: entity_type
notes: s_corp | sole_prop | W2

label: ytd_remainder_earned_income_override
notes: Optional: override the projected remainder-of-year earned income (after actual YTD earned income) instead of a linear pro-rated estimate. Leave blank to use the linear estimate.

label: business_expenses_annual
notes: Schedule C ordinary deductions

label: home_office_expenses_annual
notes: Form 8829

label: health_insurance_premiums_annual
notes: SEHI above-the-line deduction

label: qbi_eligible
notes: S.199A 20% QBI deduction

label: reasonable_salary_annual
notes: W-2 salary; FICA on this only

label: sehi_added_to_w2
notes: SEHI via W-2 box 1

label: state_corporate_surcharge_rate
notes: IL surcharge on distributable income

label: qbi_phaseout_applies
notes: True if income near S.199A phaseout

label: monthly_401k_contribution
notes: Pre-tax; Member 1 only during earned years

label: annual_401k_limit_base_year
notes: Includes age-50+ catch-up

label: ss_funding_discount_year
notes: First year Social Security gross benefits are reduced for trust-fund underfunding stress. Default 2032.

label: ss_funding_discount_pct
notes: Percentage reduction to gross Social Security benefits from the funding-discount year onward. Default 22%.

label: first_payment
notes: treat as Single annuity (terminates at Wife death); no J&S continuation despite legacy CSV note

label: dividend_rate
notes: Annual rate credited on the account base. Each year's dividend splits into two pieces: the additional_income_pct share compounds the guaranteed payment for life, and the rest (1 - additional_income_pct) pays out as cash on top of the guaranteed payment until Recovery Age.

label: additional_income_pct
notes: Share of each year's dividend that is reinvested to permanently raise the guaranteed payment (compounds every future year). The remaining share pays out as cash income until Recovery Age. Applies from the first-distribution year (= first_payment year); before that year the dividend is 100% reinvested.

label: deferral_years
notes: Contract years before first_payment where 100% of the dividend is reinvested (no income paid yet). During this period the guaranteed payment grows by dividend_rate x 0.55 (deferral_dampening) per year.

label: qualified
notes: Employer pension plan. Qualified (IRA-sourced) annuities are fully taxable each year; non-qualified annuities use exclusion_ratio to exclude a basis-recovery portion.

label: initial_guaranteed_income_payment
notes: Guaranteed monthly payment in the first-distribution year; grows over time as reinvested dividends (additional_income_pct) compound it.

label: payout_type
notes: Fixed | Variable | COLA — Monte Carlo behavior only (no effect on the deterministic base plan): Fixed: payment unchanged; Variable: scaled by sampled portfolio return vs expected; COLA: scaled by sampled inflation vs expected.

label: first_payment
notes: non-qualified; terminates at Wife's death

label: base
notes: Starting account-value base. dividend_rate is credited on the actuarial reserve derived from this base each year (reserve_factor anchors the starting reserve), and the reinvested share (additional_income_pct) compounds the guaranteed payment.

label: dividend_rate
notes: Annual rate credited on the account base. Each year's dividend splits into two pieces: the additional_income_pct share compounds the guaranteed payment for life, and the rest (1 - additional_income_pct) pays out as cash on top of the guaranteed payment until Recovery Age.

label: additional_income_pct
notes: Share of each year's dividend that is reinvested to permanently raise the guaranteed payment (compounds every future year). The remaining share pays out as cash income until Recovery Age. Applies from the first-distribution year (= first_payment year); before that year the dividend is 100% reinvested.

label: deferral_years
notes: Contract years before first_payment where 100% of the dividend is reinvested (no income paid yet). During this period the guaranteed payment grows by dividend_rate x deferral_dampening per year. Contract years 10-14 before income (2029).

label: reserve_factor
notes: Fraction of base that anchors the starting actuarial reserve (reserve_start = base x reserve_factor). The reserve then decays/grows on a fixed calibration curve over the payout years and drives both the compounding guaranteed payment and the cash dividend. Calibrated to 2025 illustration.

label: deferral_dampening
notes: Dampens guaranteed-payment growth during the deferral period (growth rate = dividend_rate x deferral_dampening per deferral year). Lower values mean slower guaranteed-payment growth before income starts.

label: qualified
notes: Personal market. Qualified (IRA-sourced) annuities are fully taxable each year; non-qualified annuities use exclusion_ratio to exclude a basis-recovery portion.

label: exclusion_ratio
notes: Taxable fraction of each non-qualified payment (the rest is treated as tax-free return of basis/principal). Only applies when qualified = FALSE. Per illustration (age 68: $3330/$4511).

label: initial_guaranteed_income_payment
notes: Guaranteed monthly payment in the first-distribution year; grows over time as reinvested dividends (additional_income_pct) compound it.

label: payout_type
notes: Fixed | Variable | COLA — Monte Carlo behavior only (no effect on the deterministic base plan): Fixed: payment unchanged; Variable: scaled by sampled portfolio return vs expected; COLA: scaled by sampled inflation vs expected.

label: first_payment
notes: qualified - RMD applies; continues to surviving spouse at js_pct

label: js_pct
notes: applies to all Joint annuities and pension; survivor receives full payment for life

label: principal_recovery_age
notes: Age (of the relevant annuitant) at which the original principal is treated as fully recovered. Before this age, each stream pays its guaranteed payment PLUS a cash dividend (the un-reinvested share of the yearly dividend); from this age onward the cash dividend stops and only the guaranteed payment continues. Also used as the payoff horizon in the Net Worth sheet's death-benefit calc.

label: index_401k_limit
notes: Index 401(k) contribution limit after base year.
